category_title: Non-ambulant adolescent with Rett Syndrome and profound learning difficulties communicating via eye gaze and vocalizations
minimal_context: Evie is transitioning back to the classroom to join her class after being in the pool.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Evie, we are back from the pool, so it is time to join the class.

STUDENT: (Makes sharp, small noises)

TUTOR: Let's go inside.

STUDENT: (The noises progressively become longer and louder)

TUTOR: We have to join the others now.

STUDENT: (Starts to cry, continuing to cry for 19 minutes)

options.A: Walk Evie around the school for a 'change of scenery'.
options.B: Ignore Evie due to her undesirable behavior during the transition.
options.C: Do not allow the crying to continue once it has been addressed.
options.D: Allow Evie to withdraw from the transition to the classroom.
